questions.question: Which of the following CSS properties is inherited by default?
questions.explanation: The color property is inherited by default, meaning child elements will use the parent's color value unless overridden. Properties like border, margin, and padding are not inherited by default.

questions.question: What does the 'inherit' keyword do when applied to a CSS property?
choices: Resets the property to the browser default
Forces the property to take its parent's computed value
Sets the property to its initial specification value
questions.explanation: The inherit keyword forces a property to take the computed value from its parent element. This works for any property, even those that are not normally inherited like border or padding.

questions.question: What does the 'unset' keyword do?
choices: Always resets to the initial value
Acts as inherit for inherited properties and initial for non-inherited properties
Removes the property from the cascade
questions.explanation: The unset keyword behaves as inherit if the property is naturally inherited (like color or font-size), and as initial if the property is not naturally inherited (like border or margin).

questions.question: What does the 'all' shorthand property affect when used with the 'initial' keyword?
choices: Every CSS property except direction and unicode-bidi
questions.explanation: The all shorthand property resets every CSS property except direction and unicode-bidi. Using all: initial sets all properties to their specification-defined initial values.

questions.question: The ___ keyword resets a CSS property to the default value defined in the specification, ignoring inheritance.
questions.explanation: The initial keyword sets a property to its initial value as defined by the CSS specification. For example, the initial value of display is inline, regardless of what the parent's display value is.

questions.question: The ___ shorthand property can be used to reset all CSS properties on an element at once.
questions.explanation: The all shorthand property represents all CSS properties except direction and unicode-bidi. It can be set to inherit, initial, unset, or revert to reset an element's styles in one declaration.
